items.mistake: Using Ward linkage with non-Euclidean distance metrics
items.fix: Pair Ward linkage with Euclidean distance on appropriately scaled features, or switch linkage (e.g. average/complete) when using cosine or other metrics.
items.why: Ward's method is defined in terms of squared Euclidean distances; mixing it with arbitrary metrics breaks the variance-minimization interpretation and can yield poor merges.
items.example: scikit-learn raises an error if `linkage='ward'` with `metric='cosine'`; use `linkage='average'` and `metric='cosine'` instead.

items.mistake: Clustering on raw features with different units
items.fix: Standardize or scale features (e.g. z-score) before computing distances so one large-scale column does not dominate merges.
items.why: Hierarchical clustering uses pairwise distances; unscaled income in thousands can overwhelm age in years.
items.example: After `StandardScaler().fit_transform(X)`, Ward linkage balances recency, frequency, and monetary RFM scores instead of letting monetary values drive every merge.

items.mistake: Running hierarchical clustering on millions of rows without subsampling
items.fix: Subsample, use k-means or mini-batch methods for coarse structure, or cluster prototypes; reserve hierarchical clustering for moderate n.
items.why: Storing and updating an n×n distance matrix explodes memory and runtime beyond laptop-scale datasets.
items.example: On 500k users, cluster 2k stratified samples hierarchically, then assign full population labels with a faster method trained on centroids.
